description:
Implement push --task ID for the issues plugin. Behavior depends on
the task's status and whether it has a stored
external.github_issues.number:

- status=open or in_progress, no number stored → POST /repos/o/n/issues
  with title `"<title> [bl-xxxx]"` and body=description; store
  {number, url, source:"balls", synced_at:now} in external.github_issues.
- status changed, number stored → PATCH /repos/o/n/issues/N with new
  title/body/state.
- status=closed, number stored → PATCH with state=closed.
- status=closed, no number stored → noop (don't open an issue just to
  immediately close it).
- Any task without status changes since synced_at → noop (return {}).

WHY: idempotency is the contract for a participant whose push runs on
every subscribed event. The `source:"balls"` marker is the loop-avoidance
hinge — B4a's pull half reads this to recognize "this is our own write
coming back via the GH API." Without it, a balls push immediately
followed by a sync pulls the just-pushed state back in as if it were a
GH-side change.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mockito-driven unit tests for each branch above.
- Concurrent push of the same task → server gets one create (verified
  by mockito hit count).
- A failure response (rate limit, 422) surfaces as a structured error
  with the GH message; non-zero exit; balls core's failure-policy layer
  decides what to do.
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file (split into
  push.rs + push_tests.rs if needed).
title: Push half: balls -> GH issue create/update/close (idempotent)
